授業目的: This course surveys Japanese religions from ancient times to the present from the perspectives of doctrine, institution, and relation with the state. The first half of the course consists of a historical review. The second half is devoted to an examination of the question of religion-state relation through case studies.
到達目標: By taking this course, students will gain knowledge in the history and belief system of Japanese religions, familiarity with key concepts for the study of Japanese religions, and insights into current issues of a religious nature in Japan.

学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
